question_text: Three identical containers are filled with different liquids: liquid A (density 0.8 g/cm³), liquid B (density 1.0 g/cm³), and liquid C (density 1.3 g/cm³). If a wooden block with density 0.95 g/cm³ is placed in each container, in which liquid(s) would the block float?
explanation: An object floats when its density is less than the liquid's density. The wooden block (0.95 g/cm³) is less dense than liquid A (0.8 g/cm³) is false—it's MORE dense, so it sinks. Wait: 0.95 < 0.8? No. The block floats only in liquids less dense than itself. Only liquid A (0.8 g/cm³) is less dense than the block's 0.95 g/cm³, so it floats in A only.
